$comment: UDS factory build version — the factory's OUTPUT vintage, distinct from per-file lastUpdated and the contract-block format version (v1). See .cursor/rules/uds-factory-versioning.mdc. Version format YYYY.MM.DD.N; changelog newest-first; drift compares by changelog position, not string math. `labels` is the human-readable registry of affects[] tags; the drift pass carries a real detector per label.

changelog.summary: Contract block gains two required sections and a currency gate. (1) Summary — a 1-2 sentence plain-language 'what this is / does', sits right under Factory-version. (2) Depends on — the other UDS components this one relies on, with the relationship: structural deps (a udc-* nested as an instance) AND behavioral deps (a component this one opens/reveals, e.g. date-picker -> udc-calendar 'opens in popover'). Variant axes is now formalized in the template too. (3) Currency: the block is a DERIVED VIEW of the live component — any section can go stale, not just States. New Phase C check 12 verifies the machine-checkable half against the actual component set every run: Variant axes must match componentPropertyDefinitions verbatim (this is the metric-card Hover-vs-Hovered drift), States baseline must cover the State axis, Parts must name real regions, Depends on (nested half) must match the nested udc-* instances, and Factory-version: must equal the plugin-data stamp. The prose half (Summary, Events, Keyboard, SR, Acceptance, behavioral Props) is presence-checked and kept current by a new discipline: touching a component regenerates the WHOLE block, never one line, then re-stamps (factory-versioning 'Touching a component regenerates its contract'). Every component built before this version is flagged behind — its block is missing Summary/Depends on and may carry stale variant/state lines; regenerate and re-stamp. Pre-versioning drafts with no block at all (calendar) get one authored on first touch.

Grid-cell focus-ring exception (design-language §6.4). A focusable cell tiled into a dense repeating grid — calendar day cells and analogous small tiled subcomponents — may carry its focus indicator as an EDGE RING (a 2px stroke bound to outline-focus-visible on the cell box, no offset, no separate ring frame) instead of the canonical gapped offset overlay. Reason: a 36px day cell on a 4px grid gap has no room for a 2px-gap + 2px-ring offset without colliding with its neighbour. Narrow carve-out: grid cells only — a normal inline control (button, field, link, checkbox, trailing icon button) still uses the gapped offset, and an edge ring there remains the thickened-border anti-pattern the focus-ring gate catches. Phase C check 8 now recognizes a bound-outline-focus-visible edge stroke on a grid cell as conforming. Surfaced from the calendar review; calendar day cells stamped to this bar with the edge ring documented, not rebuilt.

Field & control metrics contract ratified (design-language §10). Every form control — text input, search, dropdown, combobox, text area, date picker, checkbox, radio, toggle — shares one set of metrics: a 48px alignment/touch container with the control vertically centered, 14px input text on text fields (font-size-base, default scale), label = nested udc-label default (14px), 10px helper (font-size-xs, default scale), 4px label/control and control/helper gaps. Load-bearing idea: what aligns is the CONTAINER, not the visible control — a text field fills the 48px box; a checkbox (18px), radio (20px), toggle (52x32) center a smaller control inside the same 48px container (never a 48px-tall checkbox). This also closes a touch-target gap: checkbox/radio hit areas are ~24px and toggle ~32px today, under the 44px minimum. Density and font scale (larger/default/smaller) are user/app settings and mobile is form-level layout — none is a per-control build decision the factory authors; author at default scale. New Phase C check 11 flags controls whose container is off the contract, a visible control stretched to the container height, or label/helper styles/gaps off the contract. Form controls built before this version are flagged behind — re-size their container and center the visible control. Figma source-of-truth gaps surfaced not auto-fixed: checkbox/radio/toggle container sizes, the label/base-medium 14-vs-16 style drift, and the text-area 6px outer gap. [Corrected 2026-06-09: field px are default-scale resolutions — input 14 / helper 10 / label 14, not the larger-scale 16/12 this entry first stated; the contract always meant bind-the-token. See design-language §10 and token-architecture 'Don't claim a token value — resolve it first'.]

Two construction contracts from the combobox 2026-06-08 review. (1) Icon-tone split — color an icon by what it means. Content/contextual icons (leading/search glyph) follow the full tone: Error->icon-error, Disabled->icon-disabled, Read-only->icon-secondary. Control affordances (chevron, clear, stepper caret) follow usability not validity: they stay neutral/icon-interactive in Error (a red chevron is wrong), grey to icon-disabled when Disabled, and hide/mute in Read-only. Rule of thumb: Error tone splits the icons (content follows, controls don't); Disabled tone hits everything. The combobox shipped every icon pinned to icon-interactive across all states. (2) Field popover overlay — a field's open menu/listbox is an absolute overlay anchored to the field's bottom edge (clipsContent=false on the variant), not an in-flow sibling after the helper row; the combobox open state wedged the helper between the field and its menu (field bottom y72 -> helper y76 -> menu y92). Also enforced: read-only fields sit on surface-subtle (already design-language §3.2 canon) — the combobox read-only had mirrored Default. Phase C check 6 now splits content vs control adornments, and new check 10 flags in-flow (non-overlay) open menus. Fields with icons or popovers built before this version are flagged behind — re-tone their icons per state and re-anchor their open menu.

Field labels nest udc-label AND forward its properties. This supersedes the 2026.06.09.2 'host-owned label' direction, which was wrong — replacing the nested label with raw TEXT threw away udc-label's editable text / required / optional / leading-icon / right-slot (badge) properties and would force rebuilding them in every field. The standard is now: nest udc-label, then make its properties reachable by the consumer via isExposedInstance=true (or hoist text/required to top-level host props), and drive the nested label's tone from host state (Error->tone=Error, Disabled->tone=Disabled). The earlier size drift (paragraph/sm-medium 12px vs label/base-medium 14px) was the draft udc-label binding the wrong text style, not a nesting problem — fixed in the label component. New Phase C label-forwarding gate flags raw-text field labels, nested-but-unexposed labels (the original combobox defect), and labels whose tone doesn't track state. Labeled fields built before this version (text-input, search drafts that nest udc-label without forwarding) are flagged behind — expose the nested label's properties.

[SUPERSEDED by 2026.06.09.3 — do not build to this] Briefly required form components to OWN their field label as host-owned TEXT instead of nesting udc-label. Reverted same day: host-owned labels strand udc-label's text/required/icon/badge property surface. The real fix is nest-and-forward (see .3).

Hardened the focus-ring gate from presence-only to construction-verified: the offset ring MUST use STRETCH/STRETCH constraints (never MIN, which pins it at a fixed size), be parented to the focused element box (not the variant wrapper), overlay via negative inset without changing the box footprint, and have an unclipped ancestor chain up to and including the component set. Focusable components built before this version are flagged behind — re-verify the ring resizes and isn't clipped.

Canonical state names moved to the adjective form (Hover->Hovered, Focus->Focused) and gained Dragged + Indeterminate conditional states; new Phase C naming-convention gate flags any non-canonical variant/state value. New runs emit the canonical names; components built under 2026.06.07.1 are flagged for rename + re-stamp.

First versioned factory. Gapped offset focus ring; per-variant tone-bearing adornment rebinding; secondary-affordance ghost emphasis + contextual default glyph; behavioral props captured in the contract block; nested-instance exposure coverage for every reachable instance; state-vs-kind axis hygiene.

labels.tone-axis: component set has a Tone variant axis
labels.focusable: component has a focus / focus-visible state
labels.labeled-field: component is a labeled field/control whose label must be a nested udc-label instance with its properties forwarded (exposed or hoisted), not raw text and not unexposed
labels.iconized-states: component with content and/or control icons across Error/Disabled/Read-only states — content icons follow tone (Error->icon-error, Disabled->icon-disabled, Read-only->icon-secondary), control affordances follow usability (neutral in Error, icon-disabled when Disabled, hidden/muted in Read-only)
labels.field-popover: component opens a menu/listbox/popover that must be an absolute overlay anchored to the field's bottom edge (combobox, select, dropdown, autocomplete, date-picker), never an in-flow sibling after the helper row
labels.form-control: component is a field or form control (text input, search, dropdown, combobox, text area, date picker, checkbox, radio, toggle) that must conform to the design-language §10 field & control metrics contract — 48px alignment/touch container with the visible control centered, shared label/helper styles and gaps
